q: Every reaction has a ___, or speed, at which it proceeds.
Different chemical reactions occur at different ___ (speeds).
An explosion of dynamite takes place within a ___.
___ of rocks and rusting of an iron pipe happen for years.
Depletion of the ozone layer depends on the relative rates of reactions that produce and destroy ___ molecules.
___ action is essential in food preservatives.
The economical synthesis of ___ to produce fertiliser depends on the rate at which gaseous nitrogen and hydrogen can be converted to ammonia.
Knowledge of reaction rates is required in drug design, pollution control, and ___ processing.
The study of reaction rates is known as "___".
___ is a measure of how fast or how slow the reaction is.
To describe the quantitative measurement of a reaction rate, it is necessary to specify how fast the reactants are ___ up or the products are formed.
An iron nail reacts ___ in the air as it rusts.
White ___ bursts into flames when exposed to air.
Candle wax burns only after the ___ of the candle is lighted.
The rate of reaction varies greatly for different reactions and under different ___.
The rate of reaction means measuring the change in the amount of a product produced or a reactant used up in the ___ taken for that change.
The reaction rate is usually expressed as the change in the amount of a reactant used up or the product formed in a unit of ___.
For the progress of a simple reaction ($Reactant A \\rightarrow Product B$), the rate can be illustrated as the ___ in concentration of reactant A with time.
Magnesium reacts with dilute ___ acid giving off hydrogen gas.
The volume of hydrogen gas released in the process of reaction at time ___ can be measured.
The rate of a reaction can be expressed as the change in the ___ (mass, volume or concentration) of reactants or products per unit of time.
If the substance is a gas, the amount consumed or formed is expressed in ___ units or as measured in volume.
___ units are used if the reactant or product is in solution.
The time measurement is expressed in whatever units fit the reaction (seconds, minutes, hours, days, months or ___).
In chemical reactions, the rate of a reaction in solution is measured in terms of a ___ in the concentration of the reactant or an increase in the concentration of the product per unit time.
The delta ($\\Delta$) means ___ and the square bracket $[ ]$ represents molar concentration.
The minus ($-$) sign means a ___ in the concentration of the reactant.
The plus ($+$) sign means an ___ in the concentration of the product.
Rate is expressed as a ___ quantity.
For a general chemical reaction $aA + bB \\xrightarrow{k} cC + dD$, $k$ is the ___ constant.
The rate constant explains the ___ between the molar concentration of the reactants and the rate of a chemical reaction.
The ___ ratios from the balanced chemical equation can be used to determine the rates of change in concentrations of products and reactants.
The unit for the rate of a chemical reaction in solution is $mol \\ dm^{-3} \\ s^{-1}$.
___ theory explains how chemical reactions occur.
Collision theory is based on the kinetic ___ theory of gases.
Reactant particles moving in random directions possess ___ energy.
When reactant particles frequently collide with one another, the ___ is transferred as a result of the collision.
Not all ___ between reactant particles result in the formation of reaction products.
For a reaction to occur, colliding particles must impact with certain ___ energy known as an activation energy.
During a collision, the ___ orientation of the reactant molecules is important to determine whether or not a reaction occurs.
If the colliding particles have the ___ orientation, they will bounce off each other.
The unsuccessful collisions with ___ energy do not produce a reaction.
When the particles collide with the correct orientation, they must have enough energy for a ___ collision.
Reacting particles must collide with sufficient energy to form the ___ complex.
The activated complex is a ___ species formed by the reactant particles as a result of the collision.
It is postulated that chemical reactions depend on the collisions between the reacting particles — atoms, ___ or ions.
Reacting particles must collide in the correct orientation with sufficient energy to overcome the energy ___ to react.
According to the collision theory, a reaction will speed up if the ___ of collision increases.
A reaction will speed up if the ___ of particles with energy that is greater than the activation energy increases.
Collision theory can be used to explain main factors such as concentration, temperature, pressure, and ___ size.
The colliding molecules must possess at least a certain ___ energy necessary to rearrange the original bonds.
The activation energy ($E_a$) is the minimum energy that reactant particles must possess for a successful ___ to take place.
During the progress of a reaction, an unstable intermediate which is known as an ___ complex is formed.
A hydrogen molecule and an iodine molecule collide to form an unstable intermediate $[H_2I_2]$.
In order to get the product $HI$, the reactants must pass through a short-lived, high-energy ___ state.
The activation energy represents an energy ___ for the reaction.
In an exothermic reaction, the final product $HI$ has ___ energy than that of the reactants.
When there is sufficient energy to overcome the energy barrier, the formation of the ___ is favourable.
A high $E_a$ means that relatively ___ collisions have the required energy to produce the activated complex.
With a low $E_a$, ___ collisions have sufficient energy to react, and the reaction rate is fast.
___ energy is the minimum amount of energy that reacting particles must have to form the activated complex.
___ state is a highly energetic state that would be highly unstable.
Successful collision is a collision that causes a ___ reaction.
Increasing the ___ of the reactants increases the rate of reactions for many reactions involving liquids or gases.
If more molecules or ions are in the reaction area, there is a greater chance of more ___ collisions per unit time.
Zinc granule reacts fairly ___ with $1.0$ mol $dm^{-3}$ dilute hydrochloric acid.
In reactions involving solids, a reaction occurs at the ___ surface between reactants.
The rate of a reaction involving a solid reactant is increased by increasing the ___ area of the solid.
The ___ the particle size, the larger the surface area will be.
If the surface is ___, it will be hit by more moving reacting particles per unit time.
Powdered aluminium of the same mass reacts ___ in the cold.
Reducing the size of particles increases the surface area and increases the number of ___ per second.
Large pieces of ___ are difficult to ignite whereas small pieces burn more rapidly.
Changing the ___ does not affect the rate of reaction that only involves solids or liquids.
Pressure affects the rate of a reaction involving ___.
Increasing the pressure increases the ___ of the reaction.
The pressure is ___ proportional to the volume of a gas at a constant temperature.
At ___ pressure, the volume becomes larger and the number of collisions is smaller.
At high pressure, the volume becomes ___, and the number of collisions is greater.
Food can cook faster in a ___ cooker than in a pot.
Trapped ___ becomes hotter as the pressure increases, causing more water molecules to collide.
Most chemical reactions react ___ at a high temperature than at a low temperature.
The ___ energy of a gas is directly proportional to the temperature.
Increasing the temperature means that the molecules move ___ and collide more frequently.
The number of ___ collisions per second of the reacting particles increases with temperature.
The time required to hard-boil an egg is much shorter at $100$ °C than at $80$ °C.
Foods are cooled or frozen to slow down the chemical reactions that result in the ___ of food.
The energy distribution curve is also known as the ___ distribution.
The area under the Boltzmann curve represents the ___ of particles within a certain energy range.
Chemical reactions take place only if the collided particles possess ___ energy, $E_a$.
When the temperature is increased, the shape of the Boltzmann distribution curve ___.
Generally, for a ___ degrees rise in temperature, the rate of reactions approximately doubles.
A ___ is a substance which alters the rate of a chemical reaction but remains chemically unchanged.
Catalysts that make chemical reactions go faster are known as ___ catalysts.
Iron is used in the ___ Process for manufacturing ammonia.
Vanadium(V) oxide or platinised ___ is used in the Contact Process.
Manganese(IV) oxide is used in the preparation of ___ from hydrogen peroxide.
A catalyst used to slow down a chemical reaction is known as a ___ catalyst.
Alcohol acts as a catalyst to slow down the ___ of sodium sulphite to sodium sulphate.
Catalysts provide an alternative ___ for the reaction with lower activation energy.
Catalysts may be ___, that is, uniformly mixed with the reactants.
___ catalysts are in different phases than the reactants.
Homogeneous catalysts most often catalyse gaseous and ___ reactions.
In the Lead chamber process, ___ oxide acts as a catalyst.
Most heterogeneous catalysts are ___ that catalyse gaseous or liquid reactants.
In the Ostwald process, ___ catalyst speeds up the reaction.
___ act as biocatalysts for specific biochemical reactions in living systems.
The enzyme ptyalin is found in ___.
The enzyme pepsin is found in ___ juice.
The reaction rate using enzymes is much faster than ___ catalysts.
Some chemical reactions obtain the energy they need from ___.
Reactions that are initiated by light are called ___ reactions.
The ___ the light, the faster is the reaction.
When the reactant molecules absorb the light ($hv$), a ___ reaction takes place.
Photosynthesis in green plants is a ___ reaction.
Silver ___ decomposition in photographic films is a photochemical reaction.
The reaction between hydrogen and ___ is extremely slow in the dark.
Hydrogen and chlorine reaction becomes ___ when exposed to intense sunlight.
A ___ is a portion of a physical state that is homogeneous throughout.
Storage of preserved foods at a ___ temperature in a refrigerator is effective for slowing bacterial decay.
At high altitudes with low temperature, it takes a ___ time to boil an egg.
Corrosion ___ like imidazoline prevent $H_2S$ and $CO_2$ corrosion.
Volatile corrosion inhibitors (VCI) such as ___ or hydrazine prevent corrosion in boilers.
Wrapping metals in volatile corrosion inhibitor-coated ___ is a common protection against moisture.
